purpose: Deterministic exact-confirmation checkpoints for consequential PP&D DevHub boundaries. These fixtures describe guardrails only and do not authorize automation of official actions.

checkpoints.consequence: The user may be certifying that application information is true, complete, and ready for official PP&D review.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the acknowledgement or certification language in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM CERTIFICATION REVIEWED
source_basis: PP&D DevHub permit application guidance describes acknowledgement/certification review as part of authenticated application workflows.
PP&D guardrail plan classifies certification and acknowledgement as consequential official actions requiring exact confirmation.

checkpoints.consequence: A file may become part of the official permit or correction record and may trigger PP&D review activity.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the exact file list and destination in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM OFFICIAL UPLOAD
source_basis: PP&D single PDF and file preparation guidance distinguishes required application, plan, calculation, and supporting-document uploads.
PP&D upload-corrections guidance treats correction uploads as authenticated DevHub workflow steps tied to permit or application records.

checkpoints.consequence: The application may be officially submitted to PP&D, starting intake, review, status tracking, or fee workflows.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the application summary and any warnings in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM PERMIT SUBMISSION
source_basis: PP&D DevHub permit application guidance describes save-for-later behavior, required uploads, acknowledgement/certification, and post-submission status tracking.
PP&D guardrail plan classifies permit submission and trade permit purchase as consequential official actions.

checkpoints.consequence: An inspection appointment or request may be created or changed on the official permit record.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the inspection type, permit, contact details, and requested date in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM INSPECTION SCHEDULING
source_basis: PP&D online permitting tools and DevHub FAQ identify inspection scheduling as an authenticated account-scoped DevHub service.
PP&D guardrail plan classifies scheduling inspections as consequential official workflow activity.

checkpoints.consequence: The user may cancel, withdraw, void, or otherwise end an official request, permit, inspection, or workflow item.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the record and cancellation or withdrawal consequence in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM CANCELLATION REQUEST
source_basis: PP&D guardrail plan classifies cancel, withdraw, and official change actions as consequential and attended.
Authenticated DevHub workflow state changes are account-scoped and must not be executed without user attendance and exact confirmation.

checkpoints.consequence: The user may request an official deadline extension, permit reactivation, or status change that affects review or permit validity.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the deadline, status, and requested extension or reactivation in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM EXTENSION OR REACTIVATION
source_basis: PP&D process stages include cancellation, expiration, extension, and reactivation as closeout or lifecycle boundaries.
PP&D guardrail plan requires exact confirmation before consequential official status changes.

checkpoints.consequence: The user may enter financial details, authorize charges, submit payment, or create a financial record.
checkpoints.confirmation_prompt: Review the fee notice, amount, and payment page in DevHub. To proceed yourself, type exactly: CONFIRM PAYMENT STEP
source_basis: PP&D fee payment guidance describes payment-detail entry and final submit-payment actions as financial workflow steps.
PP&D guardrail plan prohibits automating payment-detail entry or final payment execution.
